name: Stehlik 2021 (IBM) -- tunable-coupler CZ, all 11 pairs (Table I)

provenance.citation: J. Stehlik, D. M. Zajac, D. L. Underwood, T. Phung, J. Blair, S. Carnevale, D. Klaus, G. A. Keefe, A. Carniol, M. Kumph, M. Steffen, O. E. Dial, "Tunable Coupling Architecture for Fixed-Frequency Transmon Superconducting Qubits", Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 080505 (2021)
provenance.notes: Fixed-frequency transmons coupled by a flux-tunable bus (BBQ); CZ activated by tuning the bus to turn on an effective ZZ/exchange interaction. Table I summarizes 11 qubit pairs. EVERY number below is quoted verbatim from Table I: Coupling g_Q,bus/2pi (MHz), qubit-qubit Detuning (MHz), Average T1 (us), Average T2 (us), Gate Time (ns), Error Per Gate (EPG) and Error Per Clifford (EPC) from interleaved randomized benchmarking. 'Average' T1/T2 are the per-pair averages over the two qubits as the paper reports them; we apply each to BOTH qubits symmetrically (only one value per pair is published). The T2 measurement convention is not stated in the text; its magnitude (35-151 us) is consistent with a Hahn-echo T2 for fixed-frequency transmons. The headline gate is pair 1: 46 ns, EPG 0.0015+/-0.0001 (F_avg=99.85%). Absolute qubit frequencies and anharmonicities are NOT published per pair, so the gradpulse comparison uses the ANALYTIC coherence floor (2 t_g/5) sum_q(1/T1+1/Tphi), which depends only on the published T1/T2 and gate time -- not the GRAPE floor, which would require assumed Hamiltonian parameters. Paper's own error attribution (Fig. 3 / text): at short gate times error rises from loss of adiabaticity (a COHERENT error), at long gate times from qubit decoherence -- i.e. these gates are NOT uniformly coherence-limited. Pair 9 has T2=106 > 2*T1=92 us (T2 > 2 T1 is unphysical for a single consistent measurement); gradpulse clamps its pure-dephasing rate to zero (relaxation-only floor), the same honest handling as self-inconsistent calibration entries elsewhere.
